text.title: Thread and Run Lifecycle
text.summary: Rules for creating threads, executing runs, handling all run status values, streaming, and managing resource lifecycle to avoid cost accumulation.

rule.statement: Handle all run terminal statuses explicitly: completed, failed, cancelled, expired, and requires_action.
rule.rationale: Treating only "completed" as a terminal state causes silent failures. "requires_action" means a custom function tool was called and is waiting for a response — if ignored, the run expires after 10 minutes.

rule.statement: When stop_reason is "requires_action", submit tool outputs within 10 minutes or the run expires.
rule.rationale: The Assistants API has a 10-minute timeout for tool output submission. Expired runs cannot be resumed; the entire interaction must be restarted.

rule.statement: Use order="asc" when listing thread messages to retrieve conversation order; the default (newest first) reverses the conversation.
rule.rationale: messages.list returns newest first by default. Code that iterates the result in received order reads the conversation backwards, producing incorrect context assembly.

rule.statement: Archive long threads by creating a new thread with a summary message after N turns rather than letting the thread grow indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Thread messages accumulate indefinitely. Long threads hit context limits and cost more per run because all prior messages are included in the context window.

rule.statement: Delete threads, vector stores, and files after use. Orphaned resources accumulate and are billed.
rule.rationale: OpenAI charges for stored files and vector stores independently of API calls. Unused resources from test sessions or one-off queries accumulate silently.

rule.statement: Use metadata on threads and assistants to tag with user IDs and session IDs for cost attribution.
rule.rationale: Without metadata, there is no way to attribute token costs to specific users or sessions in the usage dashboard.

example.note: run.status is checked explicitly and raises for any non-completed terminal state.

text.reference: One-shot assistant interaction with error handling
Context managers for thread and vector store lifecycle
